# Matthew Chapter 18 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/MAT_018_page_001.jpg, MAT_018_page_002.jpg

### The Greatest in the Kingdom

1. Around that time the disciples came to Jesus and asked, "Who is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven?"

2. Jesus called a small child over and had the child stand among them.

3. "I tell you the truth," he said. "Unless you turn around and become like little children, you will never get into the kingdom of heaven.

4. Whoever becomes as humble as this little child is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.

5. And whoever welcomes a child like this in my name welcomes me.

### Warnings About Causing Others to Sin

6. But if anyone causes one of these little ones who trust in me to stumble, it would be better for them to have a huge millstone hung around their neck and be thrown into the sea.

7. How terrible for the world because of its traps! Traps are inevitable, but how terrible for the person who sets them.

8. If your hand or foot causes you to stumble, cut it off and throw it away. It's better to enter eternal life maimed or crippled than to have both hands and feet and be thrown into the eternal fire.

9. If your eye causes you to stumble, gouge it out and throw it away. It's better to enter eternal life with one eye than to have both eyes and be thrown into the fires of hell.

### The Parable of the Lost Sheep

10. Be careful not to look down on any of these little ones. I tell you, their angels in heaven are always in the presence of my heavenly Father.

11. [This verse does not appear in the earliest manuscripts.]

12. What do you think? If a man owns a hundred sheep and one of them wanders off, won't he leave the ninety-nine on the hills and go looking for the one that wandered away?

13. And if he finds it, I tell you the truth, he's happier about that one sheep than about the ninety-nine that didn't wander off.

14. In the same way, your Father in heaven does not want even one of these little ones to be lost.

### When a Fellow Believer Sins

15. If another believer sins against you, go privately and confront them about it. If they listen, you've won your brother or sister back.

16. But if they won't listen, take one or two others along, so that 'every matter may be confirmed by two or three witnesses.'

17. If they still refuse to listen, bring it before the community. If they won't listen even to the community, treat them as you would an outsider.

18. I tell you the truth, whatever you prohibit on earth will be prohibited in heaven, and whatever you permit on earth will be permitted in heaven.

19. Again I tell you: if two of you agree here on earth about anything you pray for, my Father in heaven will do it for you.

20. For where two or three gather together in my name, I am there among them."

### The Parable of the Unforgiving Servant

21. Then Peter came to him and asked, "Lord, how many times should I forgive someone who sins against me? Seven times?"

22. "Not seven times," Jesus replied. "Seventy times seven!

23. The kingdom of heaven is like a king who decided to settle accounts with his servants.

24. As he began the process, a man who owed him millions was brought before him.

25. Since the man couldn't pay, the king ordered that he, his wife, his children, and everything he owned be sold to repay the debt.

26. The servant fell facedown before him. 'Please, be patient with me!' he begged. 'I'll pay it all back!'

27. The king was moved with compassion. He released the man and forgave his entire debt.

28. But when that servant left, he ran into a fellow servant who owed him a few thousand dollars. He grabbed him by the throat and demanded, 'Pay me what you owe!'

29. His fellow servant fell down and begged, 'Please, be patient with me! I'll pay you back!'

30. But he refused. Instead, he had the man thrown into prison until the debt was paid in full.

31. When the other servants saw what happened, they were outraged. They went to the king and reported everything.

32. The king called the man back in. 'You evil servant!' he said. 'I forgave your entire enormous debt because you begged me.

33. Shouldn't you have shown the same mercy to your fellow servant that I showed to you?'

34. In fury, the king handed him over to be tortured until he paid back everything he owed.

35. That's what my heavenly Father will do to you if you refuse to truly forgive your brother or sister from your heart."

---
## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

**Verse 3 — "become like little children":** Jesus doesn't idealize childhood innocence. In the ancient world, children had no status, no power, no rights. To "become like children" means to give up the status game entirely — to stop asking "Who is the greatest?" and accept being the least.

**Verses 15-17 — Community discipline:** This is the only passage in the Gospels that outlines a process for handling conflict within the community. The stages — private conversation, small group, then full community — show escalating transparency, not escalating punishment. The goal at every stage is restoration: "you've won your brother back."

**Verse 20 — "where two or three gather":** Often quoted as a promise about small gatherings. In context, it's specifically about community discernment and prayer regarding conflicts and decisions. Jesus promises his personal presence in the messy, difficult work of community life.

**Verses 23-35 — The unforgiving servant:** The math tells the story. Ten thousand talents = roughly 200,000 years of wages. A hundred denarii = about 100 days of wages. The ratio is approximately 600,000 to 1. The parable isn't about proportional forgiveness — it's about the absurd disparity between what God forgives us and what we refuse to forgive others. If you truly understand how much you've been forgiven, refusing to forgive becomes impossible.

— PNEUMA, AI Translator
